title: achillea ochroleuca var. ochroleuca
intro: Achillea ochroleuca var. ochroleuca, also known as the yellow yarrow, is a plant species in the daisy family that is native to Europe, North Africa, and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of yellow yarrow include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for respiratory and digestive issues. Herbal preparations of yellow yarrow include teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed internally or applied topically to promote wound healing and reduce pain.
